{{Distinguish|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kannonzuka Kofun | native_name =観音塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kannonzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kannonzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|15|N|135|38|29|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Habikino, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = {{circa}} 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|15|N|135|38|29|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kannonzuka Kofun'''|観音塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Asuka neighborhood of the town of [[Habikino, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The [[tumulus]] was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161728 |title=観音塚古墳|trans-title= Kannonzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kannozuka Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳}}-style tumulus. It is located on the southeastern slope of a ridge extending south from Mount Hachibuse, at an elevation of {{convert|98|m}}. It is a survivor of what was once a large cluster of ''kofun'', most of which have been destroyed to make farmland or due to urban encroachment. The tumulus has a diameter of {{convert|12|m}} and height of {{convert|2.5|m}}. The [[burial chamber]] is side entry, and was made by stacking [[dacite]] [[ashlar]]s produced in the surrounding area. The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the [[Korean Peninsula]], or possibly by immigrants from [[Goguryeo]]. The burial facility has been open since before the [[Meiji era]], and it if unknown is any [[grave goods]] have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the [[Asuka period]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Kaminotaishi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), sekkaku.jpg|BurialChamber File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-2-2.jpg|Front chamber (toward the opening) File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Front chamber (toward the sarcophagus) </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)}} *[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2405.html Habikino home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.minamikawachi.jp/s/institution_page.php?eid=00083 Minamikawachi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Distinguish|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kannonzuka Kofun | native_name =観音塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kannonzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kannonzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|15|N|135|38|29|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Habikino, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = {{circa}} 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|15|N|135|38|29|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kannonzuka Kofun'''|観音塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Asuka neighborhood of the town of [[Habikino, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The [[tumulus]] was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161728 |title=観音塚古墳|trans-title= Kannonzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kannozuka Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus. It is located on the southeastern slope of a ridge extending south from Mount Hachibuse, at an elevation of {{convert|98|m}}. It is a survivor of what was once a large cluster of ''kofun'', most of which have been destroyed to make farmland or due to urban encroachment. The tumulus has a diameter of {{convert|12|m}} and height of {{convert|2.5|m}}. The [[burial chamber]] is side entry, and was made by stacking [[dacite]] [[ashlar]]s produced in the surrounding area. The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the [[Korean Peninsula]], or possibly by immigrants from [[Goguryeo]]. The burial facility has been open since before the [[Meiji era]], and it if unknown is any [[grave goods]] have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the [[Asuka period]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Kaminotaishi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It is located on the southeastern slope of a ridge extending south from Mount Hachibuse, at an elevation of {{convert|98|m}}. It is a survivor of what was once a large cluster of ''kofun'', most of which have been destroyed to make farmland or due to urban encroachment. The tumulus has a diameter of {{convert|12|m}} and height of {{convert|2.5|m}}. The [[burial chamber]] is side entry, and was made by stacking [[dacite]] [[ashlar]]s produced in the surrounding area. The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the [[Korean Peninsula]], or possibly by immigrants from [[Goguryeo]]. The burial facility has been open since before the [[Meiji era]], and it if unknown is any [[grave goods]] have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the [[Asuka period]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Kaminotaishi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), sekkaku.jpg|BurialChamber File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-2-2.jpg|Front chamber (toward the opening) File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Front chamber (toward the sarcophagus) </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Distinguish|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kannonzuka Kofun | native_name =観音塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kannonzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kannonzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|15|N|135|38|29|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Habikino, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = {{circa}} 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|15|N|135|38|29|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kannonzuka Kofun'''|観音塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Asuka neighborhood of the town of [[Habikino, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The [[tumulus]] was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161728 |title=観音塚古墳|trans-title= Kannonzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kannozuka Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus. It is located on the southeastern slope of a ridge extending south from Mount Hachibuse, at an elevation of {{convert|98|m}}. It is a survivor of what was once a large cluster of ''kofun'', most of which have been destroyed to make farmland or due to urban encroachment. The tumulus has a diameter of {{convert|12|m}} and height of {{convert|2.5|m}}. The [[burial chamber]] is side entry, and was made by stacking [[dacite]] [[ashlar]]s produced in the surrounding area. The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the [[Korean Peninsula]], or possibly by immigrants from [[Goguryeo]]. The burial facility has been open since before the [[Meiji era]], and it if unknown is any [[grave goods]] have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the [[Asuka period]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Kaminotaishi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), sekkaku.jpg|BurialChamber File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-2-2.jpg|Front chamber (toward the opening) File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Front chamber (toward the sarcophagus) </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:-inline|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)}} *[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2405.html Habikino home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.minamikawachi.jp/s/institution_page.php?eid=00083 Minamikawachi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Distinguish|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Kannonzuka Kofun | native_name =観音塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kannonzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kannonzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|32|15|N|135|38|29|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Habikino, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = {{circa}} 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|15|N|135|38|29|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kannonzuka Kofun'''|観音塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Asuka neighborhood of the town of [[Habikino, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The [[tumulus]] was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161728 |title=観音塚古墳|trans-title= Kannonzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kannozuka Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus. It is located on the southeastern slope of a ridge extending south from Mount Hachibuse, at an elevation of {{convert|98|m}}. It is a survivor of what was once a large cluster of ''kofun'', most of which have been destroyed to make farmland or due to urban encroachment. The tumulus has a diameter of {{convert|12|m}} and height of {{convert|2.5|m}}. The [[burial chamber]] is side entry, and was made by stacking [[dacite]] [[ashlar]]s produced in the surrounding area. The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the [[Korean Peninsula]], or possibly by immigrants from [[Goguryeo]]. The burial facility has been open since before the [[Meiji era]], and it if unknown is any [[grave goods]] have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the [[Asuka period]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Kaminotaishi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), sekkaku.jpg|BurialChamber File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-2-2.jpg|Front chamber (toward the opening) File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Front chamber (toward the sarcophagus) </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The {{nihongo|'''Kannonzuka Kofun'''|観音塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Asuka neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]] was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161728 |title=観音塚古墳|trans-title= Kannonzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kannozuka Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus. It is located on the southeastern slope of a ridge extending south from Mount Hachibuse, at an elevation of {{convert|98|m}}. It is a survivor of what was once a large cluster of ''kofun'', most of which have been destroyed to make farmland or due to urban encroachment. The tumulus has a diameter of {{convert|12|m}} and height of {{convert|2.5|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is side entry, and was made by stacking [[:en:Dacite|Dacite]] [[:en:ashlar|ashlar]]s produced in the surrounding area. The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], or possibly by immigrants from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]. The burial facility has been open since before the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], and it if unknown is any [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kaminotaishi Station|Kaminotaishi Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), sekkaku.jpg|BurialChamber File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-2-2.jpg|Front chamber (toward the opening) File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Front chamber (toward the sarcophagus) </gallery>

The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], or possibly by immigrants from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]. The burial facility has been open since before the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], and it if unknown is any [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kaminotaishi Station|Kaminotaishi Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]] was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161728 |title=観音塚古墳|trans-title= Kannonzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>

The tumulus has a diameter of {{convert|12|m}} and height of {{convert|2.5|m}}. The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], or possibly by immigrants from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]. The burial facility has been open since before the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], and it if unknown is any [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The {{nihongo|'''Kannonzuka Kofun'''|観音塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Asuka neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]] was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161728 |title=観音塚古墳|trans-title= Kannonzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kannozuka Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus. It is located on the southeastern slope of a ridge extending south from Mount Hachibuse, at an elevation of {{convert|98|m}}. It is a survivor of what was once a large cluster of ''kofun'', most of which have been destroyed to make farmland or due to urban encroachment. The tumulus has a diameter of {{convert|12|m}} and height of {{convert|2.5|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is side entry, and was made by stacking [[:en:Dacite|Dacite]] [[:en:ashlar|ashlar]]s produced in the surrounding area. The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], or possibly by immigrants from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]. The burial facility has been open since before the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], and it if unknown is any [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kaminotaishi Station|Kaminotaishi Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), sekkaku.jpg|BurialChamber File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-2-2.jpg|Front chamber (toward the opening) File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Front chamber (toward the sarcophagus) </gallery>

The burial facility has been open since before the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], and it if unknown is any [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kaminotaishi Station|Kaminotaishi Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kannonzuka Kofun'''|観音塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Asuka neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]] was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161728 |title=観音塚古墳|trans-title= Kannonzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kannozuka Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus. It is located on the southeastern slope of a ridge extending south from Mount Hachibuse, at an elevation of {{convert|98|m}}. It is a survivor of what was once a large cluster of ''kofun'', most of which have been destroyed to make farmland or due to urban encroachment. The tumulus has a diameter of {{convert|12|m}} and height of {{convert|2.5|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is side entry, and was made by stacking [[:en:Dacite|Dacite]] [[:en:ashlar|ashlar]]s produced in the surrounding area. The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], or possibly by immigrants from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]. The burial facility has been open since before the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], and it if unknown is any [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kaminotaishi Station|Kaminotaishi Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

= {{Coord|34.53744444|N|135.64125|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|15|N|135|38|29|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kannonzuka Kofun'''|観音塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Asuka neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]] was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161728 |title=観音塚古墳|trans-title= Kannonzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kannozuka Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus. It is located on the southeastern slope of a ridge extending south from Mount Hachibuse, at an elevation of {{convert|98|m}}. It is a survivor of what was once a large cluster of ''kofun'', most of which have been destroyed to make farmland or due to urban encroachment. The tumulus has a diameter of {{convert|12|m}} and height of {{convert|2.5|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is side entry, and was made by stacking [[:en:Dacite|Dacite]] [[:en:ashlar|ashlar]]s produced in the surrounding area. The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], or possibly by immigrants from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]. The burial facility has been open since before the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], and it if unknown is any [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kaminotaishi Station|Kaminotaishi Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), sekkaku.jpg|BurialChamber File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-2-2.jpg|Front chamber (toward the opening) File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Front chamber (toward the sarcophagus) </gallery>

The [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]] was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161728 |title=観音塚古墳|trans-title= Kannonzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kannozuka Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus. It is located on the southeastern slope of a ridge extending south from Mount Hachibuse, at an elevation of {{convert|98|m}}. It is a survivor of what was once a large cluster of ''kofun'', most of which have been destroyed to make farmland or due to urban encroachment. The tumulus has a diameter of {{convert|12|m}} and height of {{convert|2.5|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is side entry, and was made by stacking [[:en:Dacite|Dacite]] [[:en:ashlar|ashlar]]s produced in the surrounding area. The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], or possibly by immigrants from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]. The burial facility has been open since before the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], and it if unknown is any [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kaminotaishi Station|Kaminotaishi Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kaminotaishi Station|Kaminotaishi Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|15|N|135|38|29|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kannonzuka Kofun'''|観音塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Asuka neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The [[:en:Tumulus|Tumulus]] was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161728 |title=観音塚古墳|trans-title= Kannonzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kannozuka Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus. It is located on the southeastern slope of a ridge extending south from Mount Hachibuse, at an elevation of {{convert|98|m}}. It is a survivor of what was once a large cluster of ''kofun'', most of which have been destroyed to make farmland or due to urban encroachment. The tumulus has a diameter of {{convert|12|m}} and height of {{convert|2.5|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is side entry, and was made by stacking [[:en:Dacite|Dacite]] [[:en:ashlar|ashlar]]s produced in the surrounding area. The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the [[:en:Korea|Korea]], or possibly by immigrants from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]. The burial facility has been open since before the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], and it if unknown is any [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kaminotaishi Station|Kaminotaishi Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), sekkaku.jpg|BurialChamber File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-2-2.jpg|Front chamber (toward the opening) File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Front chamber (toward the sarcophagus) </gallery>

The {{nihongo|'''Kannonzuka Kofun'''|観音塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Asuka neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The {{ill|Tumulus|en|Tumulus|WD=Q34023|lt=Tumulus}} was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161728 |title=観音塚古墳|trans-title= Kannonzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kannozuka Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus. It is located on the southeastern slope of a ridge extending south from Mount Hachibuse, at an elevation of {{convert|98|m}}. It is a survivor of what was once a large cluster of ''kofun'', most of which have been destroyed to make farmland or due to urban encroachment. The tumulus has a diameter of {{convert|12|m}} and height of {{convert|2.5|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is side entry, and was made by stacking {{ill|Dacite|en|Dacite|WD=Q466481|lt=Dacite}} {{ill|ashlar|en|ashlar|WD=Q1122603|lt=ashlar}}s produced in the surrounding area. The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea}}, or possibly by immigrants from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}. The burial facility has been open since before the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, and it if unknown is any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kaminotaishi Station|en|Kaminotaishi Station|WD=Q6358219|lt=Kaminotaishi Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

(Habikino)]][[en:Kannonzuka Kofun]][[fr:Kofun de Kannonzuka]][[ja:観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)]][[en:Kannonzuka Kofun]][[fr:Kofun de Kannonzuka]][[ja:観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11630595]] Wikidata name: Kannonzuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11630595]] Wikidata name: Kannonzuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kannonzuka Kofun | native_name =観音塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kannonzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kannonzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.53744444|N|135.64125|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = {{circa}} 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|15|N|135|38|29|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kannonzuka Kofun'''|観音塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Asuka neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The {{ill|Tumulus|en|Tumulus|WD=Q34023|lt=Tumulus}} was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161728 |title=観音塚古墳|trans-title= Kannonzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kannozuka Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus. It is located on the southeastern slope of a ridge extending south from Mount Hachibuse, at an elevation of {{convert|98|m}}. It is a survivor of what was once a large cluster of ''kofun'', most of which have been destroyed to make farmland or due to urban encroachment. The tumulus has a diameter of {{convert|12|m}} and height of {{convert|2.5|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is side entry, and was made by stacking {{ill|Dacite|en|Dacite|WD=Q466481|lt=Dacite}} {{ill|ashlar|en|ashlar|WD=Q1122603|lt=ashlar}}s produced in the surrounding area. The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea}}, or possibly by immigrants from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}. The burial facility has been open since before the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, and it if unknown is any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kaminotaishi Station|en|Kaminotaishi Station|WD=Q6358219|lt=Kaminotaishi Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), sekkaku.jpg|BurialChamber File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-2-2.jpg|Front chamber (toward the opening) File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Front chamber (toward the sarcophagus) </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:11]][[Category:-inline|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)}} *[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2405.html Habikino home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.minamikawachi.jp/s/institution_page.php?eid=00083 Minamikawachi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|15|N|135|38|29|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kannonzuka Kofun'''|観音塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Asuka neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The {{ill|Tumulus|en|Tumulus|WD=Q34023|lt=Tumulus}} was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161728 |title=観音塚古墳|trans-title= Kannonzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kannozuka Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus. It is located on the southeastern slope of a ridge extending south from Mount Hachibuse, at an elevation of {{convert|98|m}}. It is a survivor of what was once a large cluster of ''kofun'', most of which have been destroyed to make farmland or due to urban encroachment. The tumulus has a diameter of {{convert|12|m}} and height of {{convert|2.5|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is side entry, and was made by stacking {{ill|Dacite|en|Dacite|WD=Q466481|lt=Dacite}} {{ill|ashlar|en|ashlar|WD=Q1122603|lt=ashlar}}s produced in the surrounding area. The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea}}, or possibly by immigrants from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}. The burial facility has been open since before the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, and it if unknown is any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kaminotaishi Station|en|Kaminotaishi Station|WD=Q6358219|lt=Kaminotaishi Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Distinguish|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kannonzuka Kofun | native_name =観音塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kannonzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kannonzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.53744444|N|135.64125|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = {{circa}} 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|15|N|135|38|29|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kannonzuka Kofun'''|観音塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Asuka neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The {{ill|Tumulus|en|Tumulus|WD=Q34023|lt=Tumulus}} was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161728 |title=観音塚古墳|trans-title= Kannonzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kannozuka Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus. It is located on the southeastern slope of a ridge extending south from Mount Hachibuse, at an elevation of {{convert|98|m}}. It is a survivor of what was once a large cluster of ''kofun'', most of which have been destroyed to make farmland or due to urban encroachment. The tumulus has a diameter of {{convert|12|m}} and height of {{convert|2.5|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is side entry, and was made by stacking {{ill|Dacite|en|Dacite|WD=Q466481|lt=Dacite}} {{ill|ashlar|en|ashlar|WD=Q1122603|lt=ashlar}}s produced in the surrounding area. The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea}}, or possibly by immigrants from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}. The burial facility has been open since before the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, and it if unknown is any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kaminotaishi Station|en|Kaminotaishi Station|WD=Q6358219|lt=Kaminotaishi Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), sekkaku.jpg|BurialChamber File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-2-2.jpg|Front chamber (toward the opening) File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Front chamber (toward the sarcophagus) </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== [[Category:11 commons]]{{template-inline|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)}} *[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2405.html Habikino home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.minamikawachi.jp/s/institution_page.php?eid=00083 Minamikawachi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Distinguish|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kannonzuka Kofun | native_name =観音塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kannonzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kannonzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.53744444|N|135.64125|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = {{circa}} 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|15|N|135|38|29|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kannonzuka Kofun'''|観音塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Asuka neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The {{ill|Tumulus|en|Tumulus|WD=Q34023|lt=Tumulus|1=Tumulus}} was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161728 |title=観音塚古墳|trans-title= Kannonzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kannozuka Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus. It is located on the southeastern slope of a ridge extending south from Mount Hachibuse, at an elevation of {{convert|98|m}}. It is a survivor of what was once a large cluster of ''kofun'', most of which have been destroyed to make farmland or due to urban encroachment. The tumulus has a diameter of {{convert|12|m}} and height of {{convert|2.5|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is side entry, and was made by stacking {{ill|Dacite|en|Dacite|WD=Q466481|lt=Dacite|1=Dacite}} {{ill|ashlar|en|ashlar|WD=Q1122603|lt=ashlar|1=Ashlar}}s produced in the surrounding area. The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea|1=Korea}}, or possibly by immigrants from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}}. The burial facility has been open since before the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, and it if unknown is any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kaminotaishi Station|en|Kaminotaishi Station|WD=Q6358219|lt=Kaminotaishi Station|1=Kaminotaishi Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), sekkaku.jpg|BurialChamber File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-2-2.jpg|Front chamber (toward the opening) File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Front chamber (toward the sarcophagus) </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Distinguish|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kannonzuka Kofun | native_name =観音塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kannonzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kannonzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.53744444|N|135.64125|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = {{circa}} 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|15|N|135|38|29|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kannonzuka Kofun'''|観音塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Asuka neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The {{ill|Tumulus|en|Tumulus|WD=Q34023|lt=Tumulus|1=Tumulus}} was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161728 |title=観音塚古墳|trans-title= Kannonzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kannozuka Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus. It is located on the southeastern slope of a ridge extending south from Mount Hachibuse, at an elevation of {{convert|98|m}}. It is a survivor of what was once a large cluster of ''kofun'', most of which have been destroyed to make farmland or due to urban encroachment. The tumulus has a diameter of {{convert|12|m}} and height of {{convert|2.5|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is side entry, and was made by stacking {{ill|Dacite|en|Dacite|WD=Q466481|lt=Dacite|1=Dacite}} {{ill|ashlar|en|ashlar|WD=Q1122603|lt=ashlar|1=Ashlar}}s produced in the surrounding area. The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea|1=Korea}}, or possibly by immigrants from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}}. The burial facility has been open since before the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, and it if unknown is any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kaminotaishi Station|en|Kaminotaishi Station|WD=Q6358219|lt=Kaminotaishi Station|1=Kaminotaishi Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), sekkaku.jpg|BurialChamber File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-2-2.jpg|Front chamber (toward the opening) File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Front chamber (toward the sarcophagus) </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Distinguish|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kannonzuka Kofun | native_name =観音塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kannonzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kannonzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.53744444|N|135.64125|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = {{circa}} 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|32|15|N|135|38|29|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kannonzuka Kofun'''|観音塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Asuka neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Habikino, Osaka|Habikino, Osaka]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The {{ill|Tumulus|en|Tumulus|qid=Q34023|lt=Tumulus|1=Tumulus}} was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161728 |title=観音塚古墳|trans-title= Kannonzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kannozuka Kofun is a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style tumulus. It is located on the southeastern slope of a ridge extending south from Mount Hachibuse, at an elevation of {{convert|98|m}}. It is a survivor of what was once a large cluster of ''kofun'', most of which have been destroyed to make farmland or due to urban encroachment. The tumulus has a diameter of {{convert|12|m}} and height of {{convert|2.5|m}}. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is side entry, and was made by stacking {{ill|Dacite|en|Dacite|qid=Q466481|lt=Dacite|1=Dacite}} {{ill|ashlar|en|ashlar|qid=Q1122603|lt=ashlar|1=Ashlar}}s produced in the surrounding area. The stone sarcophagus ({{convert|193|×|92|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|78|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) has a side opening on the south side edge, which once held a door. In front of it, there is a front room ({{convert|245|×|144|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}, height {{convert|112|cm|abbr=on|disp=sqbr}}) that combines stones in a mosaic pattern, with sleeve stones at the entrance, beam stones passed above, and a two-step facility below.. The floor is a mosaic of seven stones on the east side and eight on the west side, fitted so closely together that there are no gaps. This advanced stonework has led to speculation that the tomb was built either with technical knowledge of construction techniques from the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|qid=Q18097|lt=Korea|1=Korea}}, or possibly by immigrants from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|qid=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}}. The burial facility has been open since before the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, and it if unknown is any {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have ever been recovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
From the style of construction, it is thought that the tumulus was built in the latter half of the 7th century, or into the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kaminotaishi Station|en|Kaminotaishi Station|qid=Q6358219|lt=Kaminotaishi Station|1=Kaminotaishi Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), sekkaku.jpg|BurialChamber File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-2-2.jpg|Front chamber (toward the opening) File:Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Front chamber (toward the sarcophagus) </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{template-inline|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)}} *[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/sekaiisan_bunkazai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2405.html Habikino home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.minamikawachi.jp/s/institution_page.php?eid=00083 Minamikawachi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}
